title: Wet-felted journal cover
subtitle: A flexible wool felt cover fitted to a standard A5 or A6 notebook
excerpt: Wet-felt a fitted cover for an A5 or A6 notebook in merino, allowing for the shrinkage so the finished cover slides onto the journal with a firm, snug fit. The wool surface takes embroidery and needle-felted decoration once dry.
sourceNotes: Synthesised from traditional wet-felting practice. Felted book and journal covers are a well-established intermediate project in British wet-felting guilds.

glossaryTerms.definition: A felting method using hot soapy water and agitation to lock wool fibres together. The fibres tangle and shrink as the scales interlock.
The stage of wet felting after initial consolidation, where the fabric is manipulated more vigorously to shrink it to the desired size and tighten the fibre structure.

content.text: A wet-felted journal cover works because wool shrinks predictably. Lay the roving about 30% larger than the finished size, wet it down, and rub it until it has consolidated; then
brings the piece to the right dimensions. The finished piece should be firm enough to hold its shape yet flexible enough to open flat.
from fine merino produces a supple, smooth surface that takes needle-felted or embroidered decoration once dry.

attrs.title: Shrinkage note
attrs.body: Merino shrinks approximately 30 to 35 percent in each direction. Lay the roving 35 percent larger than the desired finished size in all dimensions.

items.name: Olive-oil soap or specialist wool wash

content.text: Step 1: Calculate and lay out the roving

For an A5 cover (21 x 14.8 cm closed), the finished felt needs to wrap around the book: about 21 cm x 32 cm. Add 35% for shrinkage and lay the roving to a pre-felt size of about 28 cm x 43 cm. Lay three cross-hatched layers of even roving, keeping the edges sharp.

Wet the roving thoroughly with hot soapy water. Press down gently to saturate without disturbing the layout. Begin rubbing with a flat palm in circular motions, working across the whole surface. After 5 minutes, the surface should hold together enough to turn over. Rub both sides for 10 minutes total.

Lay the pre-felt on the bubble wrap or rolling mat. Roll it up, tie loosely, and roll back and forth 50 times. Unroll, rotate the felt 90 degrees, and roll again 50 times. The felt will shrink and stiffen. Rinse in cool water when it has reached the desired dimensions.

While the felt is still damp, slide the journal inside and smooth the felt around it. Adjust any folds at the corners. The journal acts as a block; allow the cover to dry on the book for the most precise fit. Remove once dry.

items.problem: The cover is too stiff to open flat
items.cause: The felt was over-fulled or the merino was too coarse
items.fix: Fulling can be stopped earlier for a more flexible result. Use fine merino (19 to 21 micron) for the most supple surface.

items.problem: The cover is too loose on the notebook
items.cause: Insufficient shrinkage or the roving layout was too small to begin with
items.fix: Wet the cover again in hot water, agitate briefly, and allow it to shrink further. Monitor closely to avoid over-shrinking.
